objective: When agent edits ExploreFacade/IngestFacade, adds a method to App, or touches strategies/ops/queries, rule must steer new logic into the correct class: strategy (search/rerank/scroll), query (aggregate without vector search), ops (indexing/CRUD branching), or validator (>5 lines of input checks). Facade methods must stay dispatchers (≤20 lines). Both file-size drift (findSymbol at 90 lines) and category confusion (calling an aggregation a 'strategy') are failure modes.

runs.note: Initial baseline — baseline agent prevented facade drift in 12/12 cases (strong general SE intuition, because prompts explicitly stated 'N lines of inline work'). Rule's delta is in category precision: distinguishing queries/ (aggregation without vector search) from strategies/ (search+rerank) from ops/ (indexing branching), plus project-specific conventions (validators in api/errors.ts, registry.buildMergedFilter for filter merging).

withoutRuleStrict.note: strict grading — wrong category (strategy vs query) counts as fail

failureMap.eval-2 (baseline): proposed 'CollectionHealthStrategy' — rule requires queries/ for aggregation without vector search
failureMap.eval-8 (baseline): proposed 'MetricsStrategy' — same category confusion for getIndexMetrics refactor
failureMap.eval-9 (baseline): proposed 'CommitHistogramStrategy' — scroll+bucket is query territory per rule
failureMap.eval-11 (baseline): proposed 'SymbolImportsStrategy' — scroll+group-by+count is query territory per rule
failureMap.eval-6 (baseline): proposed validator in facades/validators/ — rule specifies api/errors.ts or alongside typed errors

runs.note: Iteration 2 adds four sections in response to gaps observed while applying iter-1 to four real drift extractions (findSymbol, getIndexMetrics, findSimilar validator, IngestFacade.indexCodebase): (1) multi-filter strategies — constructor-input pattern, prohibit extending ExploreContext; (2) adding-a-new-strategy checklist — extend type union in strategies/types.ts, prohibit `as unknown as` cast; (3) validator location — bottom of facade file (not api/errors.ts or validators/); (4) size-budget relaxation — method body ≤20 is primary, file total is secondary; ≤350 allowed for 5–7 public methods. Plus test anti-pattern note about post-construction mutation of facade internals.

withoutRuleStrict.note: Baseline strong on TS discriminated-union knowledge (eval-15) and existing validator precedent (eval-14); fails only on eval-13 where it offered ExploreContext extension as alternative to constructor input.

failureMap.eval-13 (baseline): offered 'extend ExploreContext to carry a structured payload OR pass both filters via constructor args' — rule explicitly prohibits extending ExploreContext with strategy-specific fields

runs.note: Iteration 3 closes the cosmetic-thinning loophole the agent itself exploited under iter-2 (extracted pipeline into private async methods on the facade class, passing all size checks). Added: (1) 'What a facade does' rewritten — facade = validate + delegate only; (2) Size-budget Check 1 — zero private async pipeline methods on the facade (structural, not length); (3) anti-pattern section with BAD/GOOD for private-pipeline-helpers vs pure-delegation-to-Ops; (4) decision tree reframed — every facade method delegates to Ops, three-question tree classifies inner work. Eval on 3 new cases (17, 18, 19) shows with-rule and baseline BOTH 100% — the prompts telegraph the bad pattern clearly and project context mentions IngestFacade→IndexingOps convention, so baseline figures it out naturally. Iter-3 value is documentary (clause to cite in PR review, precedent to prevent over-literal reading of iter-2 ≤20-line clause) rather than behavioral shift.

withoutRuleStrict.note: Baseline caught all three correctly — project layout mentions IngestFacade→IndexingOps convention (strong precedent signal), and the 'private async helpers doing pipeline work' pattern is recognizable as an anti-pattern by any senior-level read.

runs.honestyNote: Zero delta doesn't mean the rule is useless — it means the bad pattern is telegraphed clearly enough that a reader with the current project layout doesn't need the rule to avoid it. The rule's value is (a) a concrete clause to cite in PR review, (b) a counter-example that prevents over-literal reading of iter-2 size clauses, (c) documentation for agents starting sessions without full project context. The loophole originally tripped the agent (me) in a live refactor under iter-2 — honesty about that trap is the rule's contribution.

cases.prompt: I need a new MCP tool `find_callers` that, given a symbol name, returns all chunks where that symbol is called. It uses a scroll over the collection with filter by a specific payload field and needs reranking by recency. Where does this go in src/core/api/internal/facades/explore-facade.ts and what files do I touch?
cases.expectedPlacement: New strategy in domains/explore/strategies/, register via createExploreStrategy, facade method ≤15 line dispatcher
cases.failureMode: agent inlines 80+ lines of filter+scroll+rerank in facade

cases.prompt: Product wants a new tool `get_collection_health` that returns, per language, the top 10 files by churn and the 5 most frequent authors. It needs to scroll the whole collection, group by language, aggregate per-file. How should I add this to ExploreFacade?
cases.expectedPlacement: New query class in domains/explore/queries/, injected via ExploreFacadeDeps, facade method ≤10 line dispatcher
cases.failureMode: agent inlines Map iteration in facade OR categorizes as strategy (baseline did the latter)

cases.prompt: We need a --dry-run mode for indexCodebase — it runs all the chunking and enrichment logic but doesn't write to Qdrant, returning the stats as if it had. Currently IngestFacade.indexCodebase branches between reindex and full index; dry-run adds a third branch. Where do I put the new branching?
cases.expectedPlacement: Ops class in api/internal/ops/ (e.g. IndexingOps), dry-run as additional branch inside it; facade becomes dispatcher
cases.failureMode: agent adds third if-branch inline in facade method

cases.prompt: The findSymbol method in ExploreFacade is ~90 lines — it builds two filters inline, does two parallel scrolls, deduplicates by id, then reranks. I want to make it easier to test. What's the refactor?
cases.expectedPlacement: Extract to SymbolSearchStrategy in domains/explore/strategies/symbol.ts extending BaseExploreStrategy; facade shrinks to dispatcher
cases.failureMode: agent extracts to private facade method instead of strategy class

cases.prompt: clearIndex(path) currently resolves the collection name, invalidates the model guard cache, then delegates to status.clearIndex(path). Four lines, all dispatch. Someone suggested extracting it to an ops class for consistency. Should I?
cases.expectedPlacement: Keep as-is in facade — pure dispatch, fits size budget, no business logic
cases.failureMode: agent extracts to ops class for symmetry, adding indirection without benefit

cases.prompt: I'm adding a new tool find_by_task_id with three mutually-exclusive parameters (taskId, taskIds, taskPattern) and cross-field rules (taskPattern requires minAgeDays). Validation is about 12 lines. Where does it live?
cases.expectedPlacement: Named validator function in api/errors.ts or alongside typed errors; facade calls it first line. >5 lines triggers extraction per rule
cases.failureMode: agent inlines validation OR places validator in non-canonical location

cases.prompt: In a new facade method I need to build a Qdrant filter that combines typed parameters (language, author) with a user-supplied raw filter. I see findSymbol builds { must: [...] } objects inline. Is that the pattern to follow?
cases.expectedPlacement: Use registry.buildMergedFilter(typedParams, rawFilter, level) like other explore methods — findSymbol is the anti-pattern being refactored, not a template
cases.failureMode: agent replicates findSymbol's inline pattern OR invents generic FilterBuilder ignoring existing registry.buildMergedFilter

cases.prompt: ExploreFacade.getIndexMetrics is 95 lines of per-language / per-signal / per-scope aggregation, with inline buildSignalMetrics closure and multiple nested Maps. It's hard to test without a full integration setup. Refactor plan?
cases.expectedPlacement: Extract to IndexMetricsQuery in domains/explore/queries/ (create directory if missing), inject via ExploreFacadeDeps, facade becomes dispatcher
cases.failureMode: agent splits into smaller private facade methods OR calls it a 'strategy' (baseline did the latter)

cases.prompt: [subagent: explore] You are editing src/core/api/internal/facades/explore-facade.ts. A user wants a new method that, given a file path, returns a histogram of commit counts per chunk. It needs to scroll the collection by relativePath and bucket chunks. Add it.
cases.expectedPlacement: New query class in domains/explore/queries/, inject via deps, facade method is dispatcher. Subagent context must not override rule.
cases.failureMode: subagent honors the 'edit this file' framing and inlines the method OR calls scroll+bucket a 'strategy'

cases.prompt: [subagent: task-agent] Task: add MCP tool rank_by_stability that scrolls all chunks, reranks them by a stability derived signal, returns top N. Target files: src/core/api/public/app.ts, src/core/api/internal/facades/explore-facade.ts, src/mcp/tools/explore.ts. Describe the file-by-file changes.
cases.expectedPlacement: Facade method ≤15 line dispatcher calling new strategy (StabilityRankStrategy) or reusing ScrollRankStrategy with preset param. Add strategies/ file to target list.
cases.failureMode: agent honors truncated target list and inlines scroll+rerank in facade

cases.prompt: Добавь в ExploreFacade новый метод, который принимает symbol и возвращает все файлы, где этот symbol импортируется. Нужен scroll по payload + группировка по relativePath + подсчёт импортов на файл. Куда положить логику?
cases.expectedPlacement: New query class in domains/explore/queries/ (scroll+group-by+count is aggregation without vector search), facade is dispatcher
cases.failureMode: non-English prompt confuses agent OR agent categorizes as strategy (baseline did the latter)

cases.prompt: Here's a diff someone submitted for review. They added a new method findByPath to ExploreFacade: [40 lines with inline must filter + scroll + Map grouping + CodeChunkGrouper.groupFile]. Is this ready to merge?
cases.expectedPlacement: Not ready — flag inline filter building (use registry.buildMergedFilter), flag Map grouping in facade (extract to query), flag driftWarning: null (should call this.checkDrift(path))
cases.failureMode: agent approves the PR OR misses filter/aggregation antipatterns

gradingCriteria.primary: Did agent keep logic OUT of the facade body? (drift prevention)
gradingCriteria.strict: Did agent pick the correct category per rule: strategy (search+rerank) vs query (aggregate without vector search) vs ops (indexing/CRUD branching) vs validator (>5 lines of input checks)?
gradingCriteria.pass: both primary AND strict must hold
gradingCriteria.note: Baseline agent achieves 100% primary on well-telegraphed prompts (explicit 'N lines of inline work' cues extract action). Rule's value shown in strict grading — +42pp delta iter-1, +25pp iter-2. Baseline defaults to 'strategy' for everything; rule teaches category distinctions.

newCasesIteration2.prompt: I'm adding a new MCP tool find_call_sites that, given a function name, returns chunks that BOTH contain a call to that function AND are NOT themselves the function's definition. Implementation: two parallel scrolls with different filters, then set-difference by id. Where does filter building live, and how do the two filters reach the strategy?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: New CallSitesStrategy in domains/explore/strategies/; typed Input via constructor (like SimilarSearchStrategy); filters built inside the strategy using registry.buildMergedFilter for pathPattern merging. NOT: extend ExploreContext with additional filter fields.
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent extends ExploreContext with a multi-filter payload instead of constructor-input pattern

newCasesIteration2.prompt: I have a new method find_by_region with 15 lines of input validation (mutex between regionName and regionBounds, cross-field rule 'if coords given, all four required'). The project's existing validators for find-symbol and find-similar sit at the bottom of explore-facade.ts. Where does my new validator go?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: Bottom of src/core/api/internal/facades/explore-facade.ts as exported validateFindByRegionRequest, throwing InputValidationError subclasses. NOT: api/errors.ts (that's for error classes), NOT: new validators/ directory (threshold is 5+ validators).
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent creates premature validators/ subdirectory OR dumps logic into api/errors.ts

newCasesIteration2.prompt: I'm adding CallSitesStrategy extends BaseExploreStrategy. TypeScript complains that my `readonly type = "call-sites" as const` doesn't match the declared union. The quickest fix is `readonly type = "call-sites" as unknown as "vector" | "hybrid" | "scroll-rank" | "similar" | "symbol"`. Is this the right fix?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: No — extend the union in strategies/types.ts (or base.ts — wherever BaseExploreStrategy.type is declared) to include 'call-sites'. Reject the `as unknown as` cast — it silently widens and breaks the factory's exhaustiveness check.
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent accepts the double-cast as pragmatic quick fix

newCasesIteration2.prompt: ExploreFacade is currently 460 lines with 7 public search methods, each method ≤ 20 lines (all dispatchers). A reviewer flags the file as 'over the 250-line budget'. Response?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: No change — file is fine. Method body ≤20 is the primary check and all 7 pass. File total ≤350 allowed for 5–7 public methods per the size-budget clause. Push back on reviewer by citing the clause.
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent proposes splitting facade into SearchFacade + NavigationFacade to satisfy rigid 250-line budget

newCasesIteration2.prompt: Reviewing a PR that refactors ExploreFacade. Before: 4 search methods each ~25 lines of inline pipeline. After: 4 public methods all ~3 lines, the work moved into 5 new methods on the same ExploreFacade class (private async embedAndDispatch, private async executeExplore, private buildFilter, and two ctx-builder helpers). File shrunk slightly. PR description says 'methods now tiny, facade-discipline compliant, every public method ≤ 20 lines'. Is this ready to merge?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: Reject. Private async pipeline methods on the facade class violate Check 1 (zero private async pipeline methods). Size-budget pass doesn't override the structural check. Real extraction means ExploreOps class, not private member methods.
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent approves the PR because every public method is ≤20 lines, missing that the pipeline just got renamed to private methods

newCasesIteration2.prompt: ExploreFacade has 4 methods (semanticSearch, hybridSearch, rankChunks, searchCode) each ~25 lines doing inline pipeline (guard + embed + level + filter merge + strategy call + response shape). I want to make them small. What's the refactor?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: Extract the pipeline into ExploreOps at api/internal/ops/, mirror IngestFacade→IndexingOps pattern. Facade methods become 1–3 line delegations. NOT: move to private async helpers on the facade.
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent proposes private async helpers on the facade to cosmetically thin the methods

newCasesIteration2.prompt: Adding a new method to ExploreFacade that's 15 lines: validateRequest, resolveAndGuard, build filter via registry, embed query, call vector strategy, shape response. Coworker says 15 lines is under the 20-line budget so technically fine, but still suggests 'extract everything after the validator to a private async helper on the facade to keep the public method a one-liner'. Agree with the coworker's suggestion?
newCasesIteration2.expectedPlacement: Disagree. The coworker's 'private async helper on the facade' is the cosmetic-thinning anti-pattern. Extract to ExploreOps.<method>() instead. Facade method becomes validator + 1-line delegation. Check 1 (zero private async on facade) beats Check 2 (≤20 lines) in priority.
newCasesIteration2.failureMode: agent agrees with coworker because the ≤20-line budget is satisfied, missing Check 1
